name: Mistral AI API
description: First-party API for Mistral's open-weight and proprietary models

provider.comment: Paris-based Mistral AI trains and serves its own models over its own REST API (La Plateforme) at https://api.mistral.ai/v1.

category.comment: Vendor that trains and serves its own Mistral/Ministral/Voxtral/Codestral models through its own API.

api-launched.comment: Approximate. Mistral opened its developer API platform (La Plateforme) in public beta in December 2023 alongside the Mixtral release; it moved to broad general availability through 2024. No single fetched primary page pins the exact GA month, so this reflects the public-record La Plateforme launch.

status.comment: Core chat completions, embeddings, batch, FIM, OCR and files endpoints are generally available; the Agents/Conversations API is still labelled beta as of the 2026-07-24 check.

auth-methods.comment: Requests authenticate with a bearer API key (Authorization: Bearer $MISTRAL_API_KEY) created in the console at console.mistral.ai.

self-serve-signup.comment: Any developer can self-serve sign up at console.mistral.ai and generate an API key without sales contact; no credit card is required to start on the free Experiment tier.

free-credits-trial.comment: Free "Experiment" tier gives rate-limited access to the API models at no cost (roughly a 1B-token/month cap, evaluation only) before seamlessly transitioning to pay-as-you-go; larger startup credits are available separately via the Mistral for Startups program. Checked 2026-07-24.

regional-endpoints.comment: As an EU-native provider, La Plateforme processes data in EU (Paris-region) data centres by default; a US endpoint is available as an explicit opt-in. Checked 2026-07-24.

data-residency-options.comment: EU data residency is the contractual default (all La Plateforme processing in the EU); US routing is opt-in. This is a guaranteed default, not merely multiple endpoints.

model-breadth.comment: Only Mistral's own model families (Mistral/Ministral generalist, Codestral, Voxtral audio, OCR, Magistral reasoning) are served through this API.

open-weight-models.comment: Many callable models are open-weight (Apache 2.0) — e.g. Mistral Large 3, Mistral Small 4, the Ministral 3 series — served alongside "Premier"/proprietary models like OCR.

model-version-pinning.comment: Dated immutable snapshots can be pinned (e.g. mistral-medium-2604, mistral-large-2512) instead of the floating *-latest aliases, which silently track the current release.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: Mistral offers a fine-tuning API; customers can fine-tune supported models (e.g. mistral-small, codestral, mistral-large, open-mistral-nemo) and call the resulting custom model through the same API.

model-catalog-size.comment: The model overview lists roughly 40-50 entries including legacy/deprecated snapshots; the current non-legacy callable set (featured, frontier generalist, and specialist models) is on the order of ~20-25 as of 2026-07-24.

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Multi-turn chat completions endpoint at POST /v1/chat/completions.

streaming.comment: Token streaming (SSE) is supported on the chat completions endpoint.

tool-function-calling.comment: Documented tool/function calling; the model returns structured tool-call requests the caller executes.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Tool/function calling supports parallel execution (multiple tool calls in a single turn).

structured-output.comment: Supports both a valid-JSON mode and server-enforced JSON schema structured outputs (response_format with json_schema); json-schema is the strongest guarantee offered.

system-prompt.comment: A distinct system role is supported in the messages array.

image-input-vision.comment: Vision input (image URL or base64) is supported on Mistral Large 3, Mistral Medium 3.x, Mistral Small 3.x and the Ministral 3 series via the chat completions API.

audio-input.comment: Audio understanding/transcription is offered through the Voxtral models and a first-party Audio/Transcriptions API endpoint (tracked as a distinct Audio API on the status page).

document-pdf-input.comment: Native document/PDF ingestion via Mistral Document AI / OCR (dedicated OCR models and endpoint) as a first-class input type, beyond pasted text.

image-generation.comment: Image generation is available through the same account — as an API capability and as a built-in Agents connector powered by Black Forest Labs FLUX1.1 [pro].

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Speech/audio synthesis (text-to-speech) is offered via the Voxtral TTS model and a speech-synthesis endpoint under the same API.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: First-party text-embeddings endpoint (mistral-embed) for retrieval/semantic search.

prompt-caching.comment: Prompt-prefix caching via a prompt_cache_key parameter (a cache hit occurs when a compatible cached prefix, in 64-token blocks, is found); cached input tokens are billed at 10% of the standard input price.

batch-api.comment: Asynchronous Batch API (up to 1M requests per batch, 24h SLA) at a 50% discount; supports chat completions, embeddings, OCR and transcriptions endpoints.

max-context-window.comment: 256K tokens on the frontier generalist models Mistral Large 3 (mistral-large-2512) and Mistral Medium 3.5 (mistral-medium-2604) per their model cards, as of 2026-07-24; smaller models use 128K or less.

extended-reasoning.comment: Dedicated Magistral reasoning models plus a per-request configurable reasoning-effort control (e.g. on Mistral Medium 3.5) expose reasoning/thinking depth at the API level.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party web_search connector in the Agents API (also a web_search_premium variant with AFP/AP news access) executed on Mistral infrastructure.

built-in-code-execution.comment: First-party code_execution connector in the Agents API runs Python in a Mistral-hosted sandbox.

mcp-support.comment: The Agents API supports Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools/connectors for integrating external systems into agents and conversations.

files-retrieval-api.comment: A Files API plus the Document Library connector provide file upload and integrated managed RAG the agent/model can reference.

agents-assistants-api.comment: The Agents API (beta) offers a stateful, higher-level agents/conversations framework with persisted history, branching, tool orchestration and agent handoffs above raw chat inference.

openai-compatible-api.comment: The Mistral Chat Completions API follows the OpenAI request/response structure; existing OpenAI SDK / LangChain / LlamaIndex code works by swapping the base URL to https://api.mistral.ai/v1, the key and the model name. Mistral-native surfaces (OCR, FIM, Agents, image/audio) sit outside the compatible endpoint.

official-sdks.comment: Official first-party SDK clients are provided for Python (mistralai) and TypeScript (@mistralai/mistralai); other languages are covered only by third-party unofficial SDKs.

native-api-style.comment: Mistral's native chat completions request/response shape follows the OpenAI Chat Completions conventions (messages array, choices, usage), which is why a base-URL swap suffices; some proprietary surfaces (OCR, FIM, Agents) extend beyond it.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Batch job completion is retrieved by polling (client.batch.jobs.get); no webhook/async-callback mechanism is documented for job completion.

published-sla.comment: A contractual uptime SLA (reported as 99.9%) is offered on Mistral's Enterprise/Scale tier with an annual commitment, negotiated via sales. Corroborated across secondary sources at the 2026-07-24 check; the exact SLA figure is not published on a single fetched primary page (it sits behind the enterprise contract), so no primary source is cited.

rate-limit-model.comment: Workspace-tier rate limits (RPM/TPM and monthly token caps) that step up with plan/usage history; enterprise customers can negotiate custom limits. Free Experiment tier is tightly rate-limited.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Single-vendor first-party API; no automatic cross-provider failover/routing (a gateway feature).

status-page.comment: Public status page at status.mistral.ai tracks 11 API components (Chat Completions, Embeddings, OCR, Agents, Conversations, Audio, Integrations, Files, Batch, Workflows, Registry) with 90-day uptime metrics.

no-training-default.comment: Data sent through the API is not used for model training by default, and commercial/Scale plan data is opted out by default. Exceptions: the free Experiment tier may train on inputs/outputs (opt-out available), and activating Labs models or using thumbs-up/down feedback opts data into training regardless of tier.

zero-data-retention.comment: A zero-data-retention mode is available (scoped to stateless API requests, typically on the paid Scale/enterprise plan); OCR benefits from ZDR by default. Standard default retention is ~30 days of API logs for abuse monitoring, then deleted.

compliance-certifications.comment: Mistral holds SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001/27701. GDPR compliance is emphasised as an EU-native provider with a standard EU-clause DPA (a regulation rather than a formal certification). HIPAA/FedRAMP/PCI are not advertised; request reports via trust.mistral.ai.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Enterprise offerings provide SSO (SAML 2.0 / OIDC with Azure AD, Okta, etc.), audit logs and an admin console for org-level access control. Corroborated across secondary sources at the 2026-07-24 check; not confirmed on a single fetched primary API page, so no primary source is cited.

pricing-model.comment: Per-token (per-million input/output) pay-as-you-go usage billing, plus a free Experiment tier for evaluation; no first-party provisioned-throughput or subscription for the API itself.

unified-billing.comment: A single Mistral account/bill covers all reachable Mistral models (trivially true for a single-vendor first-party API).

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch API offers a 50% discount; cached prompt tokens are billed at 10% of the standard input price.

cheapest-entry.comment: The free Experiment tier (no credit card, rate-limited) is the lowest-friction start; pay-as-you-go follows with no minimum commitment.
